output.final_deliverable:
Chain of Wikipedia URLs starting from Main Page:

1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents
3) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_continents
4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Africa
5) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nigeria

Verified adjacency (evidence that URL[4] links to URL[5]):
On the Africa page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Africa) the country list includes a link to Nigeria: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Africa links to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nigeria (see the Africa page country list) [source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Africa].

Each URL above was reached by following a link on the previous page: Main Page -> Portal:Contents -> List of continents -> Africa -> Nigeria. (Visited pages and link evidence are cited inline.)
output.action_summary: I started at the Wikipedia Main Page and followed links to Portal:Contents, then to List of continents, then to the Africa article, then to the Nigeria article. I verified that the Africa page links to the Nigeria page by checking the Africa article (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Africa) for the Nigeria link [source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Africa].

438219de-64cb-4a48-b806-17f101ec72b7.title:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

be4d4d36-eba1-487c-a999-26494b10dc46.title: Build a chain of ≥4 Wikipedia URLs starting from Main Page (sequential search → visit)

details.internal_plan:
Goal: produce an ordered chain of at least 4 Wikipedia article URLs where each URL appears as a link on the previous page, starting from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page. Also produce one verified adjacency pair by showing the predecessor page contains an <a href=> link to the successor, with an extracted snippet or anchor text as evidence.

Target facts to extract:
- Ordered list: chain[0] = https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page, chain[1], chain[2], chain[3], (optional chain[4+]).
- For each page visited: extract the list of internal Wikipedia links (hrefs beginning with /wiki/), and their full absolute URLs.
- For one adjacency (prefer near the end): extract the HTML or visible anchor text/snippet from URL[i] that contains a link to URL[i+1] to verify adjacency.

Likely sources:
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page (start)
- The linked Wikipedia article pages discovered on each visited page.

Verification steps (measurable):
1) Visit Main Page and extract candidate internal links; record URLs.
2) Choose one linked article (chain[1]) and visit it; extract its internal links.
3) Repeat until chain length ≥ 4.
4) For the chosen adjacency pair (preferably between chain[n-1] and chain[n]), re-visit chain[n-1] (or use extracted content) and extract the exact anchor (href and surrounding text) that points to chain[n]. Save the snippet as evidence.

Extraction intent (what to pull from each visit):
- Page URL visited
- Page title (from the HTML <title> or page heading)
- All internal Wikipedia link URLs (absolute form), with their anchor texts when available
- For the verified adjacency pair: the exact anchor tag or surrounding sentence showing the link

Measurable output fields (the final output must include):
- chain: ordered array of absolute Wikipedia URLs, length >= 4
- verification: { predecessor: URL, successor: URL, evidence_source_url: URL, evidence_anchor_text_or_snippet: string }
- visits: array of objects { url: URL, title: string, extracted_internal_links_count: integer }

Why this reduces uncertainty:
- By visiting pages and extracting link hrefs directly, we obtain definitive, auditable evidence of adjacency rather than relying on search snippets. The explicit anchor/snippet verifies that URL[i] links to URL[i+1].

Execution plan (sequential steps to run):
1) search for 'site:en.wikipedia.org Main Page Wikipedia' (intent: find main page) — but the start URL is given, so skip search and directly visit https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page.
2) visit Main Page: extract internal wiki links and pick a clear article link as chain[1].
3) visit chain[1]: extract internal wiki links and pick chain[2].
4) visit chain[2]: extract links and pick chain[3]. Continue if desired to get >4.
5) verify adjacency: re-check chain[2] (or chain[3]'s predecessor) to extract the anchor that points to chain[3] (or chain[4]).

Constraints and choices:
- Use only links found on the previous visited page (internal /wiki/ links). Avoid links to special pages (links containing ':', e.g., 'Category:' or 'File:') unless necessary; prefer standard article links.
- When selecting which link to follow, prefer the first normal article link in the main content area to keep the chain straightforward and reproducible.

Concrete next action for the executor (what to run next):
- Visit https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and extract internal article links (absolute URLs and anchor text). From that list, choose a first article link to be chain[1] and proceed sequentially as described.
extraction_fields: visits (per-visit title and internal link count)
details.verification_method: Directly extract anchor href and surrounding text from predecessor page HTML/content that points to successor; save that snippet as evidence.
details.reason_this_reduces_uncertainty: Visiting pages and extracting the actual hrefs provides incontrovertible proof of adjacency rather than relying on search metadata or memory.
details.next_step_to_execute: Visit the start URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and extract internal /wiki/ links from the main content area.
details.parent_goal:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.
